rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior nonlinear pricing work in packaged goods, and the cited 2005 JMR paper is directly about package-size quantity discounts/nonlinear pricing with structural demand and profit simulations—core constructs that overlap strongly with the citing study’s framework and research gap. The pair is a confirmed DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.2005.42.4.516), and this looks plausibly foundational/method-comparative rather than an incidental mention.
